title: HTTP/3, QUIC, QPACK, Migration, and Production Protocol Defense · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs http/3, quic, qpack, migration, and production protocol defense from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the multi-version protocol release and incident defense. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for multi-version protocol release and incident defense after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish QUIC connection IDs, encrypted packets, transport parameters, bidirectional and unidirectional streams, migration, loss recovery, and UDP path behavior.
Trace HTTP/3 control streams, SETTINGS, request streams, frame placement, cancellation, GOAWAY, and connection versus stream errors.
Explain QPACK encoder and decoder streams, dynamic-table capacity, blocked streams, acknowledgements, confidentiality, and decompression limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Production Protocol Defense
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 4014 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to stage HTTP/3 through bounded QPACK, fallback health, and explicit rollback evidence; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend standards scope, byte parsing, framing, transitions, TLS identity, intermediaries, limits, state invariants, tests, observability, version negotiation, deployment, rollback, and residual risk.

Inspect the Production Protocol Defense trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Production Protocol Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend standards scope, byte parsing, framing, transitions, tls identity, intermediaries, limits, state invariants, tests, observability, version negotiation, deployment, rollback, and residual risk. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Production Protocol Defense, keep Production Protocol Defense connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: For Production Protocol Defense, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. steps.title: Alt Svc Negotiation Fallback · changed case · Review Alt Svc Negotiation Fallback
